stories.headline: Anthropic acquires Stainless, the API tooling company
stories.blurb: Anthropic bought Stainless, a startup that auto-generates SDKs and API tooling for developers. Stainless already builds the official SDKs for Claude's API — now that team is coming in-house to make building with Claude easier across languages.
stories.why_it_matters: Better developer tools mean faster Claude adoption, especially for teams shipping production AI features.

stories.headline: Meta's new SAM 3.1 can track objects in video twice as fast
stories.blurb: Meta upgraded its Segment Anything Model to track objects in real-time video at double the speed. The new version can follow multiple things at once across a video — like tracking every person in a crowd scene or every car on a street — without slowing down. It runs faster on cheaper hardware, making video analysis more practical for everyday apps.
stories.why_it_matters: Real-time video understanding just got fast enough to run in normal applications, not just research labs.
stories.original_headline: SAM 3.1: Faster and More Accessible Real-Time Video Detection and Tracking With Multiplexing and Global Reasoning

try_this_prompt.title: The honest rewrite
try_this_prompt.prompt: I just wrote something that's technically accurate but sounds like a compliance document. Paste it below, then rewrite it so a smart 12-year-old would understand—but keep every fact intact. If you cut jargon, show me what it actually meant.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before you send the email you've been wordsmithing for 40 minutes.

daily_question: If you could pick one API or SDK to get the Stainless treatment, what would it be?
notes: Scout identified major gaps: no consumer features, no fun capability demos, no coding-agent updates beyond Stainless. Shortlist is heavy on lab partnerships and infrastructure. Top candidates beyond Anthropic/Stainless have blocking issues: Meta story includes 8,000 layoffs (scary framing), AlphaEvolve lacks concrete hook, SAM 3.1 jargon headline, co-clinician 3 weeks old. Forcing a third pick would violate 'get excited about AI' north star. Adjustments: Shipping 2 stories: (1) Anthropic acquires Stainless - solid M&A/tooling angle for business slot, (2) Need one more that passes rubric - will try SAM 3.1 with reframed angle or search for alternative

- SAM 3.1 is solid capability launch for engineer/cross

- AlphaEvolve too vague ("scaling impact" without concrete hook)

args.reason: Meta is reassigning 7,000 employees to AI-focused roles across four new organizations. Biggest internal reorg number this year - shows AI winning the budget wars inside Big Tech. Concrete scale number (7,000) makes it real. Yes, there are also layoffs mentioned, but the primary frame is internal mobilization toward AI tools and apps. Crosses business strategy and engineering org design - relevant to consultants, engineers, and anyone interested in how Big Tech pivots at scale.

args.reason: Meta's Segment Anything Model 3.1 now does real-time video detection and tracking. Despite jargon-heavy official headline, the capability is concrete and visual: AI can now track objects across live video in real-time with multiplexing. This is 'wait AI can do THAT?' for anyone who's seen SAM do image segmentation - now it works on video. Practical computer-vision win that crosses into beginner territory because seeing-is-believing with video demos. Meta's track record with SAM makes this credible.

decisions:
Initial picks strategy:
- business slot: Anthropic acquires Stainless (#8) - rare acquisition from frontier lab, SDK/tooling strategic
- beginner slot: Claude for Small Business (#10) - democratizing access, practical SMB angle
- cross slot: Need to evaluate between AlphaEvolve (#40), SAM 3.1 (#58), AI co-clinician (#41)
Meta reorg (#32) is strong but may feel like workforce sociology if layoffs are emphasized
